api/spec/packages/aip/src/currencies.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the billing currencies API: fiat and custom currency models plus list/create operations. All output compiles to v3 OpenAPI spec and SDKs via `make gen-api`; no hand-written Go or OpenAPI here.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none
patterns.description: Currency is a @discriminated union with envelope:'none' and discriminatorPropertyName:'type'. New currency variants must extend this union — never add a flat polymorphic field.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Currency { fiat: CurrencyFiat, custom: CurrencyCustom }

patterns.name: Generic base model CurrencyBase<T>
patterns.description: Type-specific models spread CurrencyBase<CurrencyType.X> and add only their own fields. Common fields (id, type, name, description, symbol) live exclusively on CurrencyBase.

patterns.name: @visibility on every field
patterns.description: All fields carry explicit @visibility(Lifecycle.*) annotations. Read-only system fields use Lifecycle.Read only; user-settable fields use Lifecycle.Create and/or Lifecycle.Read.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every exported type
patterns.description: Every model, enum, union, and scalar must have @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") to stabilize generated SDK type names and prevent collisions.

patterns.name: Stability @extension decorators on every operation
patterns.description: Every operation carries @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true) and @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true). Omitting either breaks API maturity tracking in the generated OpenAPI.

patterns.name: PagePaginationQuery spread + deepObject filter
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery for pagination and accept filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCurrenciesParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in currency.tsp, HTTP operations in operations.tsp
patterns.description: currency.tsp defines all models/enums/scalars with no HTTP imports. operations.tsp imports @typespec/http and declares all interface operations. cost-bases/ is a child folder imported via index.tsp.
patterns.example: // currency.tsp: no HTTP imports | operations.tsp: using TypeSpec.Http; interface CurrenciesCustomOperations { @post create(...) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines CurrencyType enum, Currency discriminated union, CurrencyBase<T> generic, CurrencyFiat, CurrencyCustom, and CurrencyCodeCustom scalar. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding @get/@post or importing @typespec/http breaks the model/operation separation. Missing @friendlyName causes unstable SDK names. Missing @visibility defaults all lifecycle phases.

key_file_guides.role: Declares CurrenciesOperations (list) and CurrenciesCustomOperations (create) with HTTP decorators, stability extensions, and ListCurrenciesParamsFilter model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting @extension stability decorators breaks internal API maturity tracking. Using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery causes drift from the standard pagination contract.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point that imports currency.tsp, operations.tsp, and cost-bases/operations.tsp in order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files added to this folder must be imported here; silently excluded from compilation otherwise.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`
Declaring fields without @visibility — fields default to all lifecycle phases, leaking write-only or system fields into create payloads
Adding operations without @extension(Shared.InternalExtension) and @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension) — breaks internal API maturity tracking
Using a @friendlyName that duplicates an existing model name — causes SDK type collisions
Importing @typespec/http in currency.tsp (model file) — HTTP decorators belong only in operations.tsp

decisions.decision: Currency is a discriminated union (envelope:none) rather than a flat polymorphic model
decisions.rationale: Enables SDK discriminator deserialization without a wrapper envelope field, matching the fiat/custom split at the type level.

decisions.decision: Models and operations split across currency.tsp and operations.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps model definitions free of HTTP concerns so they can be imported without pulling in HTTP decorator dependencies.
Currencies API is internal/unstable; stability decorators gate exposure in public-facing OpenAPI outputs and SDK generation.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new custom currency variant with a create operation

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer API: core customer CRUD, billing overlay, Stripe session operations, credits sub-API, and charges sub-API. Root folder coordinates sub-domain imports via index.tsp and acts as the entry point for the customers namespace.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for mutable entities
patterns.description: Core CRUD entities (Customer) spread ...Shared.Resource to inherit id, name, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at, and metadata fields consistently.

patterns.name: @visibility on every field with all applicable lifecycles
patterns.description: Each field explicitly lists every Lifecycle phase (Create, Read, Update) that applies. Omitting Update for mutable fields excludes them from PUT/upsert payloads.

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on every exported type
patterns.description: All exported models, enums, unions, and scalars must have @friendlyName prefixed with 'Billing' to stabilize generated SDK names.

patterns.name: Shared generic request/response wrappers
patterns.description: Operations use Shared.CreateRequest<T>, Shared.UpsertRequest<T>, Shared.CreateResponse<T>, Shared.GetResponse<T>, Shared.UpsertResponse<T> wrappers rather than bare model types.

patterns.name: deepObject filter + PagePaginationQuery for list operations
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery and accept filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCustomersParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in domain .tsp, HTTP operations in operations.tsp
patterns.description: customer.tsp and billing.tsp define models only with no @typespec/http imports. operations.tsp imports @typespec/http and declares all interface operations.
patterns.example: // customer.tsp: no HTTP import | operations.tsp: using TypeSpec.Http; interface CustomersOperations { @post create(...) }

patterns.name: Sub-domains as child folders imported via index.tsp
patterns.description: credits/ and charges/ sub-domains each have their own index.tsp and are imported in the parent index.tsp. New sub-domains follow the same pattern.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Customer, Address, CustomerUsageAttribution, CustomerReference, CustomerKeyReference, and UsageAttributionSubjectKey. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing @visibility on new fields defaults to all lifecycles. Not spreading Shared.Resource on new CRUD entities breaks consistency. Adding HTTP decorators here violates model/operation separation.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CustomerBillingData referencing Billing.BillingProfileReference and Apps.AppCustomerData. Bridges customer and billing/apps namespaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Imports apps and billing index.tsp — adding new billing-related customer fields here keeps billing concerns co-located. Don't put billing concerns in customer.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: Declares CustomersOperations (CRUD + list), CustomerBillingOperations (get/upsert billing, app-data, Stripe sessions), and all related request models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each operation needs @operationId and @summary. Stripe sub-operations use @route sub-paths. Error responses must include Common.ErrorResponses.

key_file_guides.role: Root import file for the customers folder; pulls in customer.tsp, operations.tsp, credits/index.tsp, charges/index.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New sub-domain folders must be imported here or they are silently excluded from compilation.

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) in customer.tsp or billing.tsp — they belong only in operations.tsp
Omitting @visibility on fields — fields default to all lifecycle phases, leaking write-only or system fields into create/update payloads
Omitting @friendlyName on new models or enums — the generated SDK name will be unstable or collide
Using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery — causes drift from the standard pagination contract
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`

decisions.decision: Customer billing and app data live in billing.tsp, not customer.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps core customer identity free of billing/app coupling; billing.tsp bridges namespaces explicitly so the dependency is traceable.

decisions.decision: credits/ and charges/ are child sub-folders imported via index.tsp
decisions.rationale: Maintains logical separation of sub-domains while keeping the TypeSpec compilation path unified through the parent index.

decisions.decision: Stripe-specific operations use @route sub-paths within CustomerBillingOperations
decisions.rationale: Co-locates Stripe session operations with the billing interface they logically belong to, avoiding a proliferation of top-level interfaces.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new customer sub-resource with list and get operations

api/client/python/examples/async.purpose: Runnable async Python example scripts demonstrating openmeter SDK usage patterns against a live endpoint; each file is self-contained and exercises one domain (customers, entitlements, ingest, meter queries, subscriptions) using the async client from openmeter.aio.

patterns.name: Async context manager client lifetime
patterns.description: Always instantiate the async Client inside `async with Client(endpoint=..., token=...) as client:` so the underlying HTTP session is properly closed even on exceptions.
patterns.example: async with Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token) as client: ...

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration with defaults
patterns.description: All connection and domain parameters read from os.environ with a fallback literal; never hardcode credentials.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: Every main() wraps all client calls in a single try/except HttpResponseError block imported from corehttp.exceptions.

patterns.name: asyncio.run entry point
patterns.description: Scripts call asyncio.run(main()) at module level with no if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union list items
patterns.description: Entitlement list items from customer_entitlements_v2.list() come back as plain dicts due to SDK deserialisation; always use .get() not attribute access.

key_file_guides.role: Shows CloudEvents ingestion: construct Event with uuid id, UTC time, specversion='1.0', then await client.events.ingest_event(event).
key_file_guides.watch_for: time must be timezone-aware (datetime.timezone.utc); omitting tzinfo silently produces a naive datetime.

key_file_guides.role: Demonstrates three entitlement sub-types (metered/static/boolean) via get_customer_entitlement_value and customer_entitlements_v2.list.
key_file_guides.watch_for: items_property list returns dicts, not typed objects — attribute access raises AttributeError.

key_file_guides.role: Meter query examples: plain total, group_by list, and advanced_meter_group_by_filters with FilterString(eq=...).
key_file_guides.watch_for: r.data may be empty; always guard len(r.data) > 0 before indexing r.data[0].

key_file_guides.role: Full subscription lifecycle: create via PlanSubscriptionCreate, get_expanded, list_customer_subscriptions with status filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: list_customer_subscriptions result pagination uses items_property not items.

key_file_guides.role: Customer CRUD: create with CustomerCreate, get by ID, update with CustomerReplaceUpdate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: client is instantiated inside the async with block, not at module level — unlike the sync counterpart.

anti_patterns: Using `from openmeter import Client` (sync) instead of `from openmeter.aio import Client` in async examples
Calling asyncio.run() inside an already-running event loop
Accessing entitlement list items with attribute syntax instead of .get() when the SDK returns dicts
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading from environment variables
Instantiating the async Client outside `async with` — HTTP session is never closed

decisions.decision: One file per domain, not a single monolithic demo
decisions.rationale: Keeps each example runnable in isolation; developers can copy a single file without unrelated dependencies.

decisions.decision: async with for client lifetime instead of explicit close()
decisions.rationale: Guarantees HTTP session teardown even when exceptions are raised inside main().

code_examples.code:
import asyncio, datetime, uuid
from openmeter.aio import Client
from openmeter.models import Event
from corehttp.exceptions import HttpResponseError

ENDPOINT: str = environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT') or 'https://openmeter.cloud'
token = environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN')

async def main() -> None:
    async with Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token) as client:
        try:
            event = Event(
                id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                source='my-app',
                specversion='1.0',
                type='prompt',
                subject='customer-1',
                time=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc),
                data={'tokens': 100, 'model': 'gpt-4o'},
            )
            await client.events.ingest_event(event)
        except HttpResponseError as e:
            print(f'Error: {e}')

asyncio.run(main())

api/client/python/examples/sync.purpose: Runnable synchronous Python example scripts mirroring the async/ folder; each script exercises one domain using the blocking `openmeter.Client` and is structurally identical to its async counterpart except for module-level client construction and absence of async/await.

patterns.name: Module-level client instantiation
patterns.description: The sync Client is instantiated at module level (outside any function), not inside a context manager, because there is no async session to manage.

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration with defaults
patterns.description: All connection and domain parameters read from os.environ with a fallback literal; never hardcode credentials.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: Every main() wraps all client calls in a single try/except HttpResponseError block imported from corehttp.exceptions.

patterns.name: Direct main() call at module level
patterns.description: Scripts call main() at module level with no asyncio.run() and no if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union list items
patterns.description: Entitlement list items from customer_entitlements_v2.list() come back as plain dicts; always use .get() not attribute access.

key_file_guides.role: Blocking CloudEvents ingestion: module-level client, then client.events.ingest_event(event) with timezone-aware datetime.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time must be timezone-aware (datetime.timezone.utc); client is module-level, not a context manager.

key_file_guides.role: Same three-type entitlement demo as async counterpart using client.customer_entitlements_v2.list and client.customer_entitlement.get_customer_entitlement_value.
key_file_guides.watch_for: items_property returns dicts; attribute access on list items raises AttributeError.

key_file_guides.role: Meter queries including FilterString(eq=...) for advanced_meter_group_by_filters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: r.data may be empty; guard len(r.data) > 0 before indexing r.data[0].

key_file_guides.role: Subscription lifecycle: create with PlanSubscriptionCreate, get_expanded, list with SubscriptionStatus filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: list result pagination uses items_property not items.

key_file_guides.role: Customer CRUD: create with CustomerCreate, get by ID, update with CustomerReplaceUpdate — all blocking calls on module-level client.
key_file_guides.watch_for: client is module-level here (unlike async/ which puts it inside async with); do not wrap in async with.

anti_patterns: Using `from openmeter.aio import Client` (async) instead of `from openmeter import Client` in sync examples
Wrapping the sync client in `async with` or calling asyncio.run()
Accessing entitlement list items with attribute syntax instead of .get() when the SDK returns dicts
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading from environment variables
Instantiating the client inside main() on every call instead of once at module level

decisions.decision: Module-level client construction instead of per-call instantiation
decisions.rationale: Connection setup is done once; repeated calls reuse the same underlying HTTP connection pool.
Developers can compare sync and async examples side-by-side; the only difference is the import path (openmeter vs openmeter.aio) and absence of await/asyncio.run.

code_examples.code:
import datetime, uuid
from os import environ
from openmeter import Client
from openmeter.models import Event
from corehttp.exceptions import HttpResponseError

ENDPOINT: str = environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT') or 'https://openmeter.cloud'
token = environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN')

client = Client(endpoint=ENDPOINT, token=token)

def main() -> None:
    try:
        event = Event(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            source='my-app',
            specversion='1.0',
            type='prompt',
            subject='customer-1',
            time=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc),
            data={'tokens': 100, 'model': 'gpt-4o'},
        )
        client.events.ingest_event(event)
        print('Event ingested successfully')
    except HttpResponseError as e:
        print(f'Error ingesting event: {e}')

main()
